text:
§ 3306 Schedules of controlled substances.

The following substances are included in the schedules of controlled substances for purposes of this article:

Schedule I. A substance in schedule I has a high potential for abuse and has no accepted medical use in treatment in the United States. The following substances are schedule I controlled substances:

(a) Opiates. Unless specifically excepted or unless listed in another schedule, any of the following opiates, including their isomers, esters, ethers, salts, and salts of isomers, esters, and ethers whenever the existence of such isomers, esters, ethers, and salts is possible within the specific chemical designation...

(b) Hallucinogenic substances. Unless specifically excepted or unless listed in another schedule, any material, compound, mixture, or preparation which contains any quantity of the following hallucinogenic substances, their salts, isomers and salts of isomers...

Schedule II. A substance in schedule II has a high potential for abuse which may lead to severe psychological or physical dependence.

(a) Any of the following substances, unless specifically excepted or unless listed in another schedule...

This section should be read in conjunction with PHL § 3302 (definitions) and PHL § 3307 (substances conditionally transferred between schedules). Reference is also made to 21 U.S.C. § 812 (federal schedules of controlled substances) and 21 U.S.C. § 802 (federal definitions).

For enforcement provisions see Penal Law § 220.00 through Penal Law § 220.65.
